meta.purpose: Draft mainstream electrical validation targets for first-generation IoTEdges products, especially IEIO-100, IER-100 and IEG-100.
meta.important_note: These are engineering targets, not confirmed datasheet specifications. Do not publish exact relay rating, analog accuracy, isolation voltage, surge/ESD level, temperature range or certification claims until schematic, component selection, PCB layout and prototype test reports confirm them.

scope.planning_basis: Mainstream industrial cabinet-mounted IoT gateway, RTU and Remote IO configurations.

protection_targets: surge protection suitable for industrial DC input
validation_methods: power-up test at minimum, nominal and maximum input voltage
power_input.seo_claim_policy: Before validation, say only that DC industrial power input is planned. Do not publish exact input range.

validation_methods: communication and IO stability test during temperature soak
temperature.seo_claim_policy: Do not publish operating temperature range until chamber or equivalent test evidence exists.

validation_methods: DIN rail clip mechanical fit check
mounting.seo_claim_policy: DIN rail mounting can be described after mechanical design is locked.

mainstream_target.isolation_target: channel-group isolation target; exact voltage TBD by circuit design

recommended_design_decisions: Decide whether dry and wet contact are supported on the same hardware or separated by SKU/jumper/software setting.
Keep pulse counting optional until firmware timing and input circuit are validated.
Expose input debounce setting in firmware if alarm/contact bounce is a target use case.
validation_methods: wet contact threshold test at low/nominal/high voltage
debounce test with mechanical contact simulation
pulse input frequency test if pulse mode is implemented
input fault and wiring mistake sanity test
acceptance_evidence: firmware debounce or pulse mode test report if implemented

seo_claim_policy.allowed_before_validation: Planned DI channels for status, alarm and contact input applications.

mainstream_target.preferred_first_generation_choice: relay output for general industrial alarm/control; transistor output can be a later or separate variant
mainstream_target.relay_rating_planning_target: 5A at 250VAC or 30VDC class relay target, subject to component and PCB validation
protection_targets: contact protection guidance in user manual
clear warning that inductive loads require external suppression

recommended_design_decisions: Decide whether DO8 means relay output, transistor output or two separate SKUs.
For SEO simplicity, use relay output as the first public positioning only after relay component and PCB clearance are validated.
Keep remote write/control disabled by default on gateway/RTU firmware until safety behavior is validated.
validation_methods: rated load switching test after component selection
endurance sample test for expected load class
failsafe behavior test after communication timeout
terminal temperature rise check under load

seo_claim_policy.allowed_before_validation: Planned DO/relay output variants for alarm and control applications.
not_allowed_before_validation: exact relay current or voltage rating

mainstream_target.accuracy_planning_target: 0.2% FS to 0.5% FS class target after calibration, subject to BOM and layout
mainstream_target.sampling_target: 1 sample/second per channel baseline; faster rates optional after validation

recommended_design_decisions: Decide whether 4-20mA and 0-10V are selectable per channel, jumper-selected or separate SKUs.
Use 4-20mA as the most important first-generation AI mode because it is common in water, energy, tank and industrial sensors.
Keep temperature input such as PT100/thermocouple as a later specialized variant.
validation_methods: calibrated 0V, 5V and 10V input test if voltage mode is implemented
noise and stability test at steady input
temperature drift test before publishing accuracy across temperature

seo_claim_policy.allowed_before_validation: Planned AI channels for 4-20mA and/or 0-10V industrial sensor signals.

mainstream_target.accuracy_planning_target: 0.5% FS class target after calibration, subject to BOM and output load

recommended_design_decisions: Treat AO as a more specialized first-generation Remote IO variant, not a required RTU baseline feature.
Decide whether voltage and current output are selectable or separate SKUs.
Validate load capability before public claims.
acceptance_evidence: prototype AO accuracy and load test report

seo_claim_policy.allowed_before_validation: Planned AO Remote IO variant for analog setpoint output applications.

role_by_product.rtu: slave and/or master depending on firmware mode

mainstream_target.isolation_planning_target: 1.5kV to 2.5kV class isolated RS485 target, subject to isolator and power design

recommended_design_decisions: Use isolated RS485 as a strong product target for industrial credibility.
Decide whether termination resistor is fixed, DIP-switch selectable or software/documentation guided.
Keep exact isolation voltage unpublished until tested.
validation_methods: Modbus communication test at each supported baud rate
multi-drop bus test with at least 3 devices
long-cable simulation or representative cable test
ESD/surge pre-compliance test before publishing protection levels
isolation test before publishing isolation voltage
acceptance_evidence: RS485 transceiver and digital isolator component selection

seo_claim_policy.allowed_before_validation: Planned RS485 Modbus RTU interface for industrial field devices.

validation_methods: local web UI access test if implemented
ESD pre-compliance test before publishing protection level
acceptance_evidence: MQTT and Modbus TCP validation report

seo_claim_policy.allowed_before_validation: Ethernet connectivity is planned for wired gateway, RTU and optional Remote IO variants.

highest_priority_checks: DO8 relay output decision and rating test
AO4 output accuracy and load test
optional light DI/AI only if included in final hardware

seo_claim_release_gates.allowed_wording: planned, designed for, target, under validation
seo_claim_release_gates.example: Designed for Modbus RTU over RS485 and industrial IO monitoring applications.

seo_claim_release_gates.allowed_wording: interface and channel count can be described internally; public exact ratings still withheld
seo_claim_release_gates.example: Internal brief may mention selected relay component, but public page should not publish relay rating yet.

seo_claim_release_gates.allowed_wording: validated channel counts and measured electrical targets can be used in product drafts
seo_claim_release_gates.example: Publish exact AI accuracy only after measured data is accepted.

seo_claim_release_gates.allowed_wording: certification marks and compliance claims can be used only after documents exist

next_actions: Review relay output strategy: relay-only first generation, transistor output variant, or both.
Decide whether AI and AO 4-20mA/0-10V modes are selectable per channel or separated by SKU.
Confirm whether isolated RS485 is mandatory for all first-generation models.
Create prototype test report templates for DI, relay, AI, AO, RS485 and Ethernet.
Update first-product-capability-validation-checklist.json after component selection or prototype evidence is available.
